steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to provide an example of a dinosaur fossil specimen's nickname. An example means naming a specific nickname — not just saying that nicknames exist or describing the specimen in other terms.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Nicknames are given to many dinosaur fossil specimens, including one housed at a museum in Denver, Colorado.: Mentions that a nicknamed specimen is in Denver but never names the nickname itself. No example. ✗
  • Choice B — A *Triceratops* fossil specimen from the Late Cretaceous period, which ended more than 65 million years ago, is housed at the Denver Museum of Nature and Science.: Describes the specimen's age and genus but omits its nickname entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — Dinosaur fossil specimens can be found at museums all over the world, and many of these specimens are given nicknames.: A general statement about nicknames existing; provides no specific example. ✗
  • Choice D — Pops is the nickname of a *Triceratops* fossil specimen housed at the Denver Museum of Nature and Science in Denver, Colorado.: Names the nickname “Pops” explicitly and identifies the specific specimen — a direct, concrete example. ✓
D is correct.
